title: First item, safely
prompt: Write safe_first(lst) that returns the first item of the list, or None if the list is empty (instead of crashing). Use try/except.

details.restate: Return the first element of the list. But if the list is empty (so there is no first element), catch the error and return None.

io.because: empty list has no first item, so return None instead of crashing

terms.meaning: the error Python raises when you ask for an item that isn't there

help.plain: Grab the first item, but if the list is empty, return None instead of erroring.
help.analogy: Reaching into a bag for the top item — if it's empty, you come back empty-handed instead of forcing it.
help.mnemonic: TRY to grab [0], EXCEPT if there's nothing there.
help.nudge: What happens if you index [0] on an empty list? Catch that specific error.
